conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Irritable. I get annoyed at the smallest things. Like if someone is talking loudly on the phone or the traffic is slow. It’s just little things, but it gets on my nerves.
Has that irritability been affecting your sleep at all?

Yeah. I lie there and think about all the things I need to do tomorrow, or I think about how I should’ve done something differently today. I can't turn my brain off. I usually just get up and watch TV until I’m too tired to stay awake.
You mentioned watching TV to fall asleep—are there any shows or hobbies you've been enjoying lately?

Not really. I used to love reading, but I haven't picked up a book in months. I just don't have the energy. I used to watch cooking shows, but even the noise of the knives and pans makes me stressed now.
You mentioned thinking about how you should've done things differently—does that happen a lot during the day too?
Constantly. Like, I'll be making a sandwich and think, "Why did I buy that bread? It's not even good." I can't stop thinking about what I did wrong or what I should've done instead.
When you think about those things you should've done differently, does it ever make you feel disappointed in yourself?
Yeah. I get really down on myself. I think, "What's wrong with me? I used to be able to handle things." Now I just feel like I'm just getting older and losing my grip.
You mentioned feeling like you're losing your grip—does that feeling ever make you want to just snap at people?
Yeah, it does. I had to bite my tongue yesterday when someone cut me off in traffic. I just wanted to scream at them, but I didn't. I just sat there and seethed.
When you're seething like that, does it feel like it takes a long time for the anger to fade?
Yeah. It hangs around for hours. I'll be doing something and suddenly remember it, and the anger will just come flooding back. It’s exhausting.
You said the anger is exhausting—does that feeling of exhaustion make it hard to get through your day?
Yeah, it makes everything harder. I just want to go to bed at 5 pm. I don't have the energy to deal with anything else.
